title: Singapore Home Sales Drop on Lack of Launches in Quiet Period
summary: Singapore home sales fell in May from the previous month as developers held back new projects ahead of the traditionally quieter mid-year period, when families travel for the school holidays.

title: Crude Tumbles as Trump Says Iran Deal Will ‘Let the Oil Flow!’
summary: Oil sank after the US and Iran agreed to an interim deal to end their months-long war, potentially allowing the Strait of Hormuz to reopen and easing a supply crunch that has rattled global energy markets.

title: Swiss Pragmatism Holds as Disputed Population Cap Rejected
summary: Swiss voters opted to play it safe on Sunday, rejecting an unprecedented proposal to cap the country’s population at 10 million people as economic risks overcame elevated concerns about immigration.

title: Billionaire Gina Rinehart Made ‘Significant’ SpaceX Investment
summary: Australia’s richest person Gina Rinehart said she made “a significant investment” in SpaceX, as her mining company laid out ambitions to work with Elon Musk’s rocket and satellite company.

title: Shipowners Seek Clarity on Hormuz Deal as Waiting Flotillas Grow
summary: A US-Iran deal intended to reopen the Strait of Hormuz within days has been met with caution by shipowners and traders, with almost all saying they would need more details in order to assess whether safe transits are possible after months of false starts.

title: Inflation Worries Keep Markets Wary Even After Iran Peace Deal
summary: Bonds and stocks rallied on Monday after a US-Iran interim deal eased nerves, though market participants warned against celebrating too soon as the conflict’s economic fallout remains unresolved.

title: London Gold Market Weighs Earlier Auction to Suit Asian Traders
summary: The London Bullion Market Association is considering moving its morning gold auction to an earlier time to accommodate traders in Asia.

title: Global Funds Retreat From Japan’s Long Bonds on Slow BOJ
summary: Little more than a year after Japan finally offered yields high enough to lure global fund managers back to its government bond market, many are starting to retreat.

title: Eyre: US Iran Deal Will Not Lead to a new Middle East
summary: US and Iran have reached an interim agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz paving the way for talks on Tehran's nuclear program and an end to a war that has engulfed the Middle East. Alan Eyre, Former Senior US Diplomat and Distinguished Diplomatic Fellow at Middle East Institute spoke to Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East and Africa on what this deal will bring to the region. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: What the US-Iran Peace Deal Means for the Strait of Hormuz
summary: No region of the world produces more oil and gas than the countries straddling the Persian Gulf. Most of this energy can only be exported aboard tankers that cross the Strait of Hormuz — a waterway that’s effectively been closed for more than three months since the US and Israel started a war against Iran in late February.

title: How the IMF's Kristalina Georgieva Hires Top Talent
summary: IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva tells Francine Lacqua the interview question she asks every candidate, reflects on a career setback that ultimately changed her life, and shares what keeps her up at night as she prepares for the next global shock. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: K-Content Powerhouse JoongAng Group Seeks Court Rehabilitation
summary: The holding company of South Korea’s JoongAng Group, a sprawling media conglomerate and K-Content powerhouse behind hits like Netflix smash Culinary Class Wars, filed for court rehabilitation, signaling a deepening liquidity crunch.

title: US and Iran Reach Deal to Reopen Hormuz
summary: The US and Iran said they reached an interim agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, halting a war that killed thousands of people and setting the stage for 60 days of negotiations on the fate of Tehran’s nuclear program. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Stocks Rally With Bonds as Iran Deal Spurs Relief: Markets Wrap
summary: Global stocks and bonds rallied while oil slumped to a three-month low after the US and Iran reached a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, sparking a relief rally across markets as concerns over energy-supply disruptions eased.

title: Treasuries Rally as Traders Trim Fed Hike Bets After Iran Deal
summary: Treasuries advanced across the curve as investors dialed back expectations for Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes following news of a deal to halt the Iran war.

title: Philippines’ Marcos Calls for Special Congress Session Amid Deadlock
summary: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. called Congress to a special session on June 17 to pass priority legislative measures, hoping lawmakers will set aside political distractions.

title: Hormuz Halt Forces Middle East Trade Into Huge Rewiring
summary: In the wake of the 12-day US-Israel and Iran war a year ago, Siemens Energy AG sent a person driving from Saudi Arabia’s Red Sea port of Jeddah across the Arabian peninsula to the industrial hub of Dammam to study an alternative route in case the crucial Strait of Hormuz was shut down.

title: Cement, Textile Stocks Biggest Winners From Pakistan Budget
summary: The government’s move to lower taxes on certain sectors including cement and textiles is likely to support related shares as Pakistan looks to balance growth and compliance with its promises to the International Monetary Fund.

title: LBMA Says Gold's Status as a Safe Haven Still Holds
summary: London Bullion Market Association CEO Ruth Crowell thinks the narrative of gold's status as a safe haven still holds. She adds that it's important to measure safe haven status in years rather than weeks or months. She speaks with Paul Allen on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Lubrizol Aims to Double India Rev. Over Next 5 Years
summary: Lubrizol Corp. President & CEO Rebecca Liebert expects to double its revenues in India over the next five years, and says the company has put a lot of investment in this market to enable that growth. She speaks with Paul Allen on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Failed SpaceX IPO Allocation Prompts Korea to Widen Mirae Review
summary: South Korea’s financial watchdog has expanded an inspection of Mirae Asset Securities Co. to examine the brokerage’s failure to secure an allocation of SpaceX shares in the company’s initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: LNG Tanker Heads Toward Hormuz as Deal Lifts Reopening Hopes
summary: A liquefied natural gas tanker that has been trapped in the Persian Gulf for over three months appears to be heading toward the Strait of Hormuz, just as the US and Iran say they have reached a deal to reopen the waterway.

title: Warsh Is Inheriting a Rates-Mountain Breakdown
summary: We’re going from a steady descent on interest rates to a potentially wild ride.

title: Stock Market Moves On From SpaceX to Focus on Iran Deal, Fed
summary: After SpaceX and its bankers pulled off the largest-ever IPO without a hitch last week, investors are returning their focus back to Earth.

title: CSIS' Cahill on US-Iran Deal Impact on Energy
summary: Ben Cahill, non-resident senior associate for the Energy Security and Climate Change Program at CSIS, says even though the US and Iran were able to come to an agreement to halt the ongoing war and reopen the strait of Hormuz, energy markets "are not quite out of the woods yet", and there is still room for prices to run up even after the MOU is signed. He speaks with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud Watts on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade". (Source: Bloomberg)

title: 'Proud Moment for Pakistan': Finance Minister on US-Iran Agreement
summary: Pakistani Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb speaks with Bloomberg Television about the "critical role" his country has played in facilitating an interim agreement between the US and Iran. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Pakistan Says It's in 'Very Good Place on Fiscal Side'
summary: Pakistan Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb believes the country is in a 'very good place on the fiscal side'. He speaks with Paul Allen on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Stocks Rally, Oil Falls on Long-Awaited Iran Peace Deal | The Asia Trade 6/15/2026
summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Korea, US Agree Cooperation on Won Weakness, Seoul Official Says
summary: South Korea and the US agreed to maintain close cooperation in addressing the won’s weakness, according to Korea’s top foreign-exchange official.

title: US and Iran Reach Deal to Halt the War, Reopen Hormuz
summary: The US and Iran reached an interim agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, halting a war that killed thousands of people and setting the stage for negotiations on the fate of Iran's nuclear program. Bloomberg's Abeer Abu Omar has the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Rises After US and Iran Agree Deal That Would Reopen Hormuz
summary: Gold rose after the US and Iran announced an interim deal to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, easing global inflation fears and potentially tempering expectations for interest-rate hikes.
